description: Master list of all classification categories used in MedRPG. This is the single source of truth for all input classification. Every player input gets classified into one of these categories or into irrelevant.

history.description: Categories for history-taking interactions via Icon 1
history.note: All history questions cost 1 AP each, even repeats

items.name: Overview / Chief Complaint
items.description: Player asks what is going on, why patient came in, chief complaint, general overview of symptoms
examples: why did you come in today

items.name: Pain — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about the pain — location, character, severity, timing, onset, duration, radiation, aggravating factors, relieving factors, quality
examples: what does the pain feel like
does the pain go anywhere else
is the pain constant or does it come and go
on a scale of 1 to 10 how bad is it
what were you doing when it started

items.name: Nausea / Vomiting — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about nausea or vomiting
examples: are you sick to your stomach
how many times did you vomit

items.name: Fever / Chills — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about fever, chills, or temperature

items.name: Appetite / Eating — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about appetite, eating, food intake
examples: have you been able to eat

items.name: Bowel Habits — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about bowel movements, constipation, diarrhea, rectal symptoms
examples: have you had a bowel movement
when was your last bowel movement

items.name: Urinary Symptoms — Detailed
items.description: Any detailed question about urination, urinary symptoms
examples: do you have to urinate more than usual

items.name: Review of Systems
items.description: Player asks if there are any other symptoms, anything else going on, general ROS sweep
examples: is there anything else going on
any chest pain or shortness of breath

items.name: Past Medical / Surgical History
items.description: Questions about past medical conditions, hospitalizations, surgeries, operations
examples: do you have any medical conditions
have you had any operations before
do you have any chronic conditions

items.name: Medications
items.description: Questions about current medications, prescriptions, supplements, OTC medications

items.name: Allergies
items.description: Questions about drug allergies, medication reactions, environmental allergies
examples: have you ever had a reaction to a medication

items.name: Social / Travel / Exposure History
items.description: Questions about social history, occupation, living situation, travel, animal exposure, sick contacts, smoking, alcohol, drug use
examples: what do you do for work
do you use any recreational drugs

items.name: Family History
items.description: Questions about family medical history, conditions that run in the family
examples: any family history of medical conditions
does anything run in your family
any genetic conditions in the family

items.name: Sexual History
items.description: Questions about sexual history, partners, activity, STIs, contraception. BLOCKED in family friendly mode.

physical_exam.description: Categories for physical examination interactions via Icon 3
physical_exam.note: Free on repeat — exam_systems_done and exam_maneuvers_done tracked in GDScript

items.name: General Appearance
items.description: General look at the patient, overall appearance, distress level
examples: what is the patient's general condition

items.name: Abdominal Examination
items.description: General abdominal exam including inspection, auscultation, palpation

items.name: Cardiovascular Examination
items.description: Heart exam, pulse, capillary refill

items.name: Respiratory Examination
items.description: Lung exam, breathing assessment

items.name: Neurological Examination
items.description: Neuro exam, mental status, reflexes

items.name: Skin Examination
items.description: Skin assessment, color, temperature, rashes

items.name: HEENT Examination
items.description: Head, eyes, ears, nose, throat examination

items.name: Musculoskeletal Examination
items.description: Joint, muscle, bone examination

items.name: Genitourinary Examination
items.description: GU exam — clinical response only, no patient dialogue

items.name: Rectal Examination
items.description: Rectal exam — clinical response only, no patient dialogue

items.name: Breast Examination
items.description: Breast exam — clinical response only, no patient dialogue

items.name: McBurney's Point
items.description: Palpation at McBurney's point for appendiceal tenderness
examples: palpate right lower quadrant at McBurney's

items.name: Rovsing's Sign
items.description: Press left lower quadrant to elicit right lower quadrant pain

items.name: Psoas Sign
items.description: Extend right hip to elicit RLQ pain

items.name: Obturator Sign
items.description: Internally rotate right hip to elicit RLQ pain

items.name: Rebound Tenderness
items.description: Release pressure to elicit rebound tenderness

items.name: Murphy's Sign
items.description: Palpate right upper quadrant during deep inspiration
examples: palpate right upper quadrant during inspiration

items.name: Other Specific Maneuver
items.description: Any other named clinical exam maneuver not listed above

items.name: General Medical Knowledge
items.description: General medical questions not specific to current case
examples: what causes right lower quadrant pain

items.name: Differential Diagnosis
items.description: Ask for differential based on findings so far. Uses conversation state to generate relevant differential.
examples: what could be causing these symptoms
what are we dealing with here

items.name: Condition — Diagnostic Tests
items.description: What tests are used to diagnose a specific condition
examples: what workup should I do for appendicitis

items.name: Condition — Treatment
items.description: What is the treatment for a specific condition
examples: what is the treatment for appendicitis
what do you do for appendicitis

items.name: Finding Significance
items.description: What does a specific finding mean clinically
examples: what does rebound tenderness tell us
what is the significance of a positive Rovsing's sign
why does the pain migrate from the belly button

irrelevant.description: Anything not clinically relevant to the encounter. Patient deflects warmly.
examples: what is the meaning of life
